artifacts.content:
From: Jordan Reyes <jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com>
To: events@district.lionsbay.ca
Date: 2026-03-18 09:42
Subject: Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls – Summer Weekend Vendor Application

Hi Lisa,

Thanks again for taking the time to chat on the phone yesterday about the summer weekend vendor spots near the Lions Bay General Store and the Village Hall parking lot. I’m sending over the details you requested for Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls.

We’re a fully enclosed 18' mobile kitchen operating under Fraser Health’s mobile food permit. Our core team for the 2026 season is 4.0 employees on payroll: myself (owner-operator/head chef), one full-time line cook, and two part-time staff who rotate window/assembly and prep, depending on volume. I’m always on site during service.

In terms of mitigating impacts on neighbours, we use a quiet Honda inverter generator mounted on vibration-dampening pads, and I avoid exterior music altogether. We do a 15–20 meter litter sweep before leaving any site and provide clearly marked garbage and recycling bins.

Because my mom, Ana Lourdes Reyes, is immunocompromised from ongoing biologic treatments for her autoimmune condition at Surrey Memorial Hospital since late 2022, I kept many of our COVID-era precautions in place. After she was hospitalized for three nights from a respiratory infection in January 2023, I formalized a written policy on February 10, 2023, requiring staff to stay home with any cold or flu symptoms, use masks during known respiratory surges (like the November 2023 RSV/COVID wave in Metro Vancouver), and sanitize high-touch surfaces every 30–45 minutes when we’re operating in close quarters.

If you need copies of our health permit, fire inspection, or written noise/litter/COVID protocols, I can send PDFs or drop them off in person. I’m aiming for Fridays–Sundays, 11:00–19:00, starting in May.

Best,

Jordan Alexander Reyes
Owner-Operator & Head Chef
Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls Inc.
North Vancouver, BC
jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
778-555-0134

metadata.subject: Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls – Summer Weekend Vendor Application

Hey Jordan,

Here’s the draft language for the Lions Bay weekend vending application. Can you review before I submit?

---
STAFFING & OPERATIONS OVERVIEW (DRAFT)

Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls will operate as a small, tightly managed mobile unit. On a typical summer service day we anticipate running with **two paid staff members on the truck at any given time**, plus the owner-operator.

• Owner-operator (you) – oversees line, expo, and customer interaction.
• One line cook – grill/fryer and hot holding.
• One service lead – assembly, POS, and window.

We’ll also have a casual “on-call” helper (likely a culinary student) who may join for peak days, but the standard operating assumption for the permit is **a three-person team max on-site**, including you. Any additional support (e.g., Elena helping with bookkeeping or your dad dropping off supplies) won’t be part of the active food-handling crew.

As discussed, I noted that all simmering and frying will be done inside the truck’s commercial kitchen under the Ansul R-102 system. I referenced that after the fire code warning and $450 penalty at Harbour View Commissary in March 2024—when Rafael left that stockpot of beans unattended—we now use the commissary strictly for cold prep and dishwashing.

Double-check that this headcount description matches how you’re actually running things this season. If you want to bump the stated number of on-truck staff up or down, let me know before I send this to the village clerk.

– Priya

Title: Summer Staffing Plan – Finalize Roles & Numbers
Date: 2026-04-10 10:00–11:30
Location: Harbour View Commissary – upstairs office
Attendees: Jordan Reyes; Elena Santos

Agenda:

1. Review projected sales and labour budget.
2. Decide on total number of regular truck staff for May–September.

Notes (draft, Jordan):

Right now I’m leaning toward **keeping it to two employees plus me** for most services. One solid line cook who can handle grill/fryer and one person on assembly/POS. I don’t think adding a third employee makes sense unless we land a big festival circuit; labour would eat the margins.

We should list the “on-call” casual as separate from the core staff count. Maybe one or two culinary students I can text for big days, but they wouldn’t be on the books as regular employees.

Remind myself to mention in any municipal paperwork that because my hood fan is pretty loud, when I’m parked in quieter spots I sometimes leave the main burners off and use a portable propane grill just outside the service window to keep things from getting too noisy inside the truck. Need to check if that’s okay with fire regs.

Also want to confirm with Elena how many evenings she’s realistically able to help with bookkeeping and social media; she’s not an employee, but her time affects whether I can get by with only **two hired staff** this summer.

Title: Call with District Health Inspector – Staffing & Kitchen Layout
Date: 2026-03-26 14:30–15:15
Location: Phone – Jordan’s cell
Attendees: Jordan Reyes; Fraser Health – Inspector Lee

Description/Prep Notes:

Goal is to walk through my operating plan for Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls before the busy season. Need to be clear about how many people are in the truck at once and how we’re using the commissary.

Talking points:

• Explain that the truck normally runs with **two staff members plus me**, not a larger crew. Emphasize that this keeps the line manageable and reduces cross-contamination risk.
• Clarify roles: I’ll float between grill, assembly, and expo; one person on hot line; one on window/POS.
• Mention that all simmering and frying now happens inside the truck under the Ansul R-102 hood. After the fire code warning at Harbour View in March 2024 (Rafael left beans unattended and we got dinged $450 plus a safety refresher), the commissary is strictly for cold prep and dishwashing.
• Confirm that we’re not planning to bring in extra temporary workers for the first part of the season; if we do later, we’ll still cap it so there are no more than **three people working inside the unit at once**.
• Ask if they have any concerns about our current headcount relative to handwashing sink access and aisle space.

Remember to have last season’s temperature logs and staff FOODSAFE certificates handy during the call.

Subject: Draft staffing blurb for Lions Bay weekend application

Hey Jordan,

Here’s a first pass at the staffing section for the Lions Bay weekend vending application. Tweak as needed before you send it to the Village office.

---

STAFFING & OPERATIONS OVERVIEW (DRAFT)

Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls will operate with a lean on-truck team. For regular weekend service, the truck typically runs with **two paid staff members on board**: Jordan Alexander Reyes (owner-operator & head chef) and one support crew member handling grill/fryer, assembly, and window duties. On peak long-weekend days we may bring in **one additional casual worker** for a short mid-day overlap to help manage lineups and speed of service.

Most of our bulk prep still happens at the Harbour View Commissary Kitchen at 1280 Marine Dr. in North Vancouver, but Jordan has shifted more of the chopping and marinating back into the truck’s prep area since November 2024, after his mom, Ana Lourdes Reyes, was diagnosed with early-stage breast cancer at Lions Gate Hospital and he started using the commissary less so he could drive her to weekday chemo sessions. Because of that, he now does a 2–3 hour early-morning prep block in the truck outside their apartment at #204 – 3175 Mountain Highway before taking food to Lions Bay.

Menu-wise, he’ll sell the regular taco lineup, bowls, kids’ quesadillas, churro bites, rice pudding, and non-alcoholic beverages, plus a once-a-month “Pay-What-You-Can” bean and rice bowl that he started outside the North Vancouver Food Bank in November 2023.

---

Let me know what to adjust.

– Marisa

artifacts.content:
From: events@sea2skyfest.ca
To: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
Date: May 3, 2026 14:02
Subject: Vendor confirmation & staffing minimums – Sea2Sky Summer Fest

Hi Jordan,

We’re excited to confirm Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls as an approved food vendor for Sea2Sky Summer Fest 2026.

A few operational details we need for our records:

1. STAFFING LEVELS
For safety and service-flow reasons, the festival requires that each truck have **no fewer than three people working during peak hours** (this can include you as owner-operator). Your application notes that you’ll be on the truck yourself with two crew members for the lunch and dinner rush, then dropping down to just you and one helper for the final hour. Please confirm this is still accurate, or advise if you’ll be adding a fourth person.

2. PREP & FOOD SAFETY
Thanks for outlining your prep process. It’s fine that most of your bulk prep is done at the commissary, and that on slower days you sometimes do some of the veggie chopping and salsa prep at home in your apartment kitchen the night before and then bring the bins to the truck in the morning, as long as everything is transported in food-grade, covered containers and held at safe temperatures.

3. MENU
We’ve recorded your menu as: signature tacos, bowls, kids’ quesadillas, churro bites, rice pudding, non-alcoholic beverages, and a once-a-month pay-what-you-can bean and rice bowl option.

Please reply by May 10 to lock in your staffing numbers.

Best,

Kara
Vendor Coordinator, Sea2Sky Summer Fest

artifacts.content:
From: permits@districtwestvan.ca
To: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
Date: March 31, 2026 11:24
Subject: Follow-up questions – Mobile Food Vendor Permit (Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls)

Dear Mr. Reyes,

Thank you for your mobile food vendor permit application for Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls.

Before we finalize, we need clarification on two points:

1. STAFFING
Your application form lists “2–3 employees on site” during operating hours, including yourself. For our records, please specify whether you intend to operate with only two people for regular days and bring in a third person only on long weekends, or if you anticipate three people as your standard crew for most lunch and dinner services. If at any point you intend to run with just a single helper, we’ll need confirmation that line management and cleanup can still be handled safely.

2. ELECTRICAL & FIRE APPROVALS
We’ve noted your comment about the electrical inspection approval from the BC Electrical Safety Authority for the generator interconnect and exterior service receptacles (Inspection Ref: E-TRK-2026-092, completed March 30, 2026), obtained after your brother Rafael did emergency rewiring in your father Miguel’s Surrey driveway when the old generator nearly sparked a fire and tripped the house breaker. Please email us a PDF copy of that inspection report.

Additionally, please briefly describe how you’ll address smoke and odour. You mentioned that since most of your menu is grilled, there will be a noticeable smoke and food smell around the truck and that you don’t have any special filtration beyond the standard hood fan, meaning nearby homes and businesses may have to tolerate some extra odour during service hours.

Kind regards,

Lena Chow
Business Licensing & Permits
District of West Vancouver

metadata.subject: Follow-up questions – Mobile Food Vendor Permit (Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls)

artifacts.content:
From: info@northshorefirepro.ca
To: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
Date: February 27, 2026 11:03
Subject: Re: Hood clearance & truck height – quote for service

Hi Jordan,

Thanks for reaching out. Based on the photos and measurements you sent, it looks like your truck’s exterior height is approximately **9 ft 11 in** from ground to the top of the main roof structure, and roughly **10 ft 4 in** to the top of the fixed exhaust housing. That’s still within the limit for most municipal bylaws we work with, but you’ll want to list the height to the main roofline (not the removable cap or any temporary signage) on most permit forms.

For our purposes, the key dimensions are:
- Interior floor-to-ceiling height near the cook line: **7 ft 1 in** at the lowest point under the hood
- Interior height at the centre aisle: **7 ft 3 in**

These clearances are adequate for safe hood access and filter removal. When we come for quarterly cleaning, our techs will bring low-profile step ladders to work comfortably under your ceiling height.

I also read your note about your COVID-19 protocols after your brother Rafael tested positive on August 6, 2022, following that long shift you worked before the family barbecue. Keeping a log of respiratory symptoms and requiring rapid tests before shifts—plus the 5-day off-truck rule—actually makes our team more comfortable working in tight spaces like your unit.

Let me know if you need a formal letter stating the approximate **9'11"** exterior height for your Lions Bay application.

Best regards,

Colin McKay
North Shore FirePro Services

metadata.subject: Re: Hood clearance & truck height – quote for service

artifacts.content:
From: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
To: planning@lionsbay.ca
Date: March 25, 2026 08:39
Subject: Re: Follow-up questions – Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls Application

Hi Kirsten,

Following up on your questions from last week:

1. Truck height
I re-measured the unit on Saturday using a proper tape and level. From the pavement to the top of the main roof panel (not including the small removable vinyl sign frame) I’m getting **roughly 9 ft 9 in** at the highest corner. The exhaust cap adds another ~5 inches but is technically a separate, removable piece. Please use **9'9"** as the height to the roofline for your records.

2. Waste management
In past locations I’ve honestly relied on customers to toss their trash in nearby public bins instead of setting up extra waste and recycling containers right at the truck, mainly to keep my setup simple. For Lions Bay, I understand that’s not ideal, so I’m planning to bring dedicated garbage and recycling totes and do a 15–20 m litter sweep before leaving.

3. Smoke/odour and health protocols
As mentioned, I don’t use open-flame grills anymore after my son’s asthma issues. I also maintain strict illness policies on the truck: anyone with respiratory symptoms is logged and we do rapid tests during outbreak periods; if someone tests positive, they stay off the truck for at least 5 days and only return after 24 hours symptom-free.

Please let me know if you need any additional clarification on the **9'9"** roof height.

Best,

Jordan

metadata.subject: Re: Follow-up questions – Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls Application

Attendees:
- Jordan Alexander Reyes

Description:

To‑do:

1. Bring: step ladder, metal tape measure, Sharpie, blue painter’s tape, notebook.
2. Park the Coastal Cove truck on level pavement by the fence.
3. Measure from ground to:
   - Bottom of serving window
   - Top of service window awning when fully extended
   - Top of main roofline

After measuring, email Alison with the confirmed height and snap a photo of the tape at the highest point for my records.

Also jot down any menu updates for the permit: tacos, bowls, churro bites, lime coconut rice pudding, plus the low‑sodium, GERD‑friendly chicken and rice bowl I’ve been making for Dad since his Surrey Memorial hypertension scare, and the dairy‑free mango agua fresca for Elena’s reflux.

artifacts.content:
From: priya.chandrasekhar@fraserhealth.ca
To: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
Date: 2026-05-03 14:17
Subject: Follow-up – Mobile Unit Plan Review & Prep Locations

Hi Jordan,

Thank you for meeting yesterday at the Surrey Fraser Health office to discuss your mobile unit and prep arrangements.

I have updated your file to reflect that your Fraser Health Authority Permit to Operate a Temporary Food Premises (Mobile Unit) was issued April 10, 2026 (Permit No. FH-MOB-44829). As we discussed, your mother Ana’s recent hepatitis B diagnosis from Surrey Memorial Hospital in March 2026 does not affect your eligibility to hold the permit, provided you maintain standard FOODSAFE practices and limit food preparation at her condo kitchen to non‑hazardous items like pre‑washing produce and assembling dry spice mixes.

One additional item: your layout drawings show the food truck as 18' long, 8.5' wide, and **approximately 9' 6" high** at the main roofline. For our records, please confirm whether the roof‑mounted exhaust cap or any signage extends above that height. The municipality’s engineering department occasionally asks us to verify total vehicle height when trucks operate near overhead wiring.

This does not affect your health permit status, but it is helpful to have the correct number on file. A simple confirmation by email is sufficient; no revised drawings are required unless the unit is modified.

Your strategies to mitigate noise, litter, and waste—especially the hourly 5‑minute litter sweep within a 20‑meter radius and transporting all commercial waste back to your North Vancouver disposal facility—are noted and appreciated. Given your past $250 warning ticket near Lonsdale Quay, your current approach should significantly reduce the risk of future enforcement issues.

Best regards,

Dr. Priya Chandrasekhar
Public Health Inspector
Fraser Health Authority

metadata.subject: Follow-up – Mobile Unit Plan Review & Prep Locations

artifacts.content:
Title: Insurance Renewal Call – Confirm Vehicle Height & Specs
Date: 2026-05-20 09:00–09:45
Location: Phone call from apartment (#204 – 3175 Mountain Highway)

Attendees:
- Jordan Alexander Reyes
- West Coast Commercial Insurance – Fleet Dept.

Description:

Agenda for call with insurance agent (re: Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls truck policy renewal):

1. Confirm VIN, plate number, and current odometer.
2. Clarify maximum insured vehicle dimensions. Their last file note lists:
   - Length: 18 ft
   - Width: 8.5 ft
   - Height: **9' 10" including roof vent**
   but my rough sketch from last year says “just under 11 ft to top of stack,” so we need to reconcile.
3. Ask whether the Honda EU7000is inverter generator and sound‑box mounting affect the declared height or any liability around noise complaints (mention the March 18, 2024 strata complaint from Mrs. Kaur in #304 about me testing the old generator in the parkade at 7:15 a.m., and the new 9:00 a.m.–8:00 p.m. no‑exceptions rule).
4. Confirm that occasional prep at Mom’s place in Surrey is acceptable given Fraser Health’s approval and Ana’s hepatitis B diagnosis being documented as non‑issue for my permit.
5. Verify coverage for off‑site events in Lions Bay, including waterfront parking lots with overhead structures.
6. Ask if adding the new low‑sodium, GERD‑friendly chicken and rice bowl and dairy‑free mango agua fresca changes anything in the product liability section.

During the call, get the agent to state clearly what height they have on file and whether they need photos or a measurement certificate. Note any changes and update my internal spec sheet afterward.

To: Lions Bay Events Committee <events@lionsbay.ca>
Date: 2026-03-18 09:12
Subject: Re: Summer Food Truck Series – Contact Details

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for considering Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls for the Summer Food Truck Series.

Here are the contact details you requested for your internal file:

Primary contact name: Jordan Alexander Reyes (Owner-Operator & Head Chef)
Preferred contact method: text or call
Daytime mobile: 778-555-9031
Backup evening line (goes to the truck when we’re on-site): 236-555-4410
Email: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com

I’m usually quickest on the 778 number, especially on service days. If you need to reach me after 8 p.m., the 236 line is better because my personal cell is often on silent once I’m home with the kids.

For the application form, you mentioned a field for “Location and description of cooking/food prep facilities.” On days when I have my kids, Mateo and Lucia, under the temporary custody schedule that started in January 2025, I do some low-risk prep work—like washing cilantro, portioning tortillas, and labelling salsa containers—at the Harbour View Commissary Kitchen at 1280 Marine Dr. between 7:00 p.m. and 9:00 p.m. while my partner, Elena, finishes her counselling sessions at the North Shore Family Services office next door. All high-risk food handling is still done in the truck’s commercial kitchen under the Type 1 hood and fire-suppression system.

Let me know if you need any other details filled in on your event application form or if you’d prefer a single phone number listed as the “applicant phone.”

Best,
Jordan
Coastal Cove Tacos & Bowls

artifacts.content:
From: Elena Santos <elena.santos@gmail.com>
To: Jordan Reyes <jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com>
Date: 2026-03-29 21:06
Subject: City form + which number are you using?

Hey love,

I was finishing up that online event application you left open on the laptop—the one for the North Shore Night Bites series—and it stopped me at the “applicant event details > applicant phone” section.

It looks like you had started typing the commissary number (604-555-1186) and then erased it. Do you want to use that, your Coastal Cove line (604-555-9820), or your cell (pretty sure it’s still 778-555-9031 unless you secretly changed it again)? The form won’t let me submit without something in that field.

Personally I’d go with your cell since you’re glued to it anyway, but I know you were saying you’d rather keep personal and business separate. If you use the Coastal Cove number, just make sure the voicemail mentions the event name so organizers don’t hang up thinking they got the wrong place.

Also, that question about where you prep food: you might want to mention that on days you’ve got Mateo and Lucia under the new schedule, you’re doing the low-risk stuff at Harbour View Commissary in the evenings while I’m next door at North Shore Family Services. It sounded like they were looking for that kind of detail.

Text me which number you decide on and I’ll hit submit before bed.

xo,
Elena

From: laura.ng@westvancouver.ca
To: jordan.reyes@coastalcovetacos.com
Date: 2026-04-23 16:18
Subject: Follow-up re: mobile unit fire inspection MV-2026-184

Hi Jordan,

Following up on your April 20, 2026 mobile food unit inspection (file MV-2026-184).

Two quick items:

1) Contact phone on file
On the inspection form you wrote 604-988-4412 as your primary contact. During our conversation at the West Vancouver fire hall you mentioned that number goes to a voicemail box you don’t always check on busy weekends, and that your personal cell (604-771-9830) is the best way to reach you while you’re operating in Lions Bay or elsewhere. For safety reasons, we strongly recommend that your application and all permits list the number you actually carry with you in the truck.

Please reply to confirm which number you want us to keep as your primary contact.

2) Extinguisher tag issue
As discussed, I did not write up a formal deficiency regarding the loose Class K extinguisher tag that your 4-year-old son Mateo pulled down during his meltdown in the truck, but I did note it informally. When we schedule the follow-up inspection prior to the busy summer season, that tag must be properly secured and out of reach of children.

Once you confirm your preferred phone number, I’ll update your file and send available dates for the follow-up inspection.

Regards,

Captain Laura Ng
District of West Vancouver Fire & Rescue Services

metadata.subject: Follow-up re: mobile unit fire inspection MV-2026-184
